openFindings.title: Root packet was closed without satisfying its own research charter
openFindings.summary: The root packet is marked complete, but its closeout tasks only document sub-phase cleanup and status alignment rather than answering RQ-1..RQ-5 with evidence, threshold recommendations, and before/after measurements.
openFindings.impact: Readers cannot rely on the root packet as the authoritative synthesis for the tuning work it claims to have completed.
openFindings.remediation: Add a root closeout synthesis that answers each research question with measured evidence, or rewrite the root spec/checklist so the narrower administrative closeout is the declared goal.

openFindings.title: The complete Level 3 root packet is missing implementation-summary.md
openFindings.summary: The packet declares `level: 3` and `status: complete`, but the root folder does not contain `implementation-summary.md` even though the repo contract requires it for every level.
openFindings.impact: The canonical continuity ladder for the root packet cannot recover a shipped implementation summary from the packet root.
openFindings.remediation: Create the missing root implementation summary and backfill it with the delivered packet-level outcome.

openFindings.title: The complete Level 3 root packet is missing decision-record.md
openFindings.summary: The root packet advertises a Level 3 closeout but does not ship the required root decision record.
openFindings.impact: Packet-level architectural decisions are not preserved at the same surface the packet uses to claim completion.
openFindings.remediation: Create the missing root decision record or downgrade the packet level if the root no longer needs Level 3 governance.

openFindings.title: Completed child packets still carry planned decision-record metadata
openFindings.summary: Child decision records in phases 001-004 still say `status: planned` even though the same child packets are marked complete and their implementation summaries report shipped work.
openFindings.impact: ADR consumers cannot trust child decision-record status fields when reviewing this packet lineage.
openFindings.remediation: Normalize the child decision-record frontmatter to `status: complete` (or archive them if no longer canonical).

openFindings.title: description.json and graph-metadata.json disagree on the packet's parent lineage
openFindings.summary: The packet description still ends its `parentChain` at `010-search-and-routing-tuning`, while graph metadata points to the current `001-search-and-routing-tuning` parent.
openFindings.impact: Lineage-aware tools can resolve the same packet through conflicting parent paths after the April 21 renumbering.
openFindings.remediation: Regenerate both metadata files from the same post-renumbering source so the parent chain and parent_id agree.

openFindings.title: graph-metadata.json still advertises a stale key-file path
openFindings.summary: Derived key files and entities still include `configs/search-weights.json`, while the packet spec and the live tree use `mcp_server/configs/search-weights.json`.
openFindings.impact: Metadata-driven navigation can point reviewers to a dead path instead of the real search weights file.
openFindings.remediation: Regenerate graph metadata so the derived key-file/entity path matches the live `mcp_server/configs/search-weights.json` surface.

openFindings.title: The packet-local deep-research prompt still points to a legacy packet path and open charter
openFindings.summary: The packet's own prompt still invokes the pre-renumbering spec path and still describes an unresolved 15-iteration research agenda after the packet root was closed as complete.
openFindings.impact: Anyone replaying the packet-local prompt is directed at the wrong packet path and a stale execution plan.
openFindings.remediation: Retire or rewrite the prompt so it points at the current packet path and reflects the closed packet state.
